  1101→@websocket_api.websocket_command(
  1102→    {
  1103→        vol.Required("type"): "lock_code_manager/set_lock_usercode",
  1104→        vol.Required(ATTR_LOCK_ENTITY_ID): str,
  1105→        vol.Required(ATTR_CODE_SLOT): int,
  1106→        vol.Optional(ATTR_USERCODE): str,
  1107→    }
  1108→)
  1109→@websocket_api.async_response
  1110→async def set_lock_usercode(
  1111→    hass: HomeAssistant,
  1112→    connection: websocket_api.ActiveConnection,
  1113→    msg: dict[str, Any],
  1114→) -> None:
  1115→    """
  1116→    Set or clear a usercode on a lock slot.
  1117→
  1118→    If usercode is provided, sets the code. If usercode is empty or not provided,
  1119→    clears the code slot.
  1120→
  1121→    This is intended for managing unmanaged slots directly on the lock.
  1122→    For LCM-managed slots, use the entity services instead.
  1123→    """
  1124→    lock_entity_id = msg[ATTR_LOCK_ENTITY_ID]
  1125→    code_slot = msg[ATTR_CODE_SLOT]
  1126→    usercode = msg.get(ATTR_USERCODE, "").strip()
  1127→
  1128→    lock = hass.data.get(DOMAIN, {}).get(CONF_LOCKS, {}).get(lock_entity_id)
  1129→    if not lock:
  1130→        connection.send_error(
  1131→            msg["id"],
  1132→            websocket_api.const.ERR_NOT_FOUND,
  1133→            f"Lock {lock_entity_id} is not managed by Lock Code Manager",
  1134→        )
  1135→        return
  1136→
  1137→    try:
  1138→        if usercode:
  1139→            # Set the usercode
  1140→            await lock.async_internal_set_usercode(code_slot, usercode)
  1141→        else:
  1142→            # Clear the usercode
  1143→            await lock.async_internal_clear_usercode(code_slot)
  1144→        connection.send_result(msg["id"], {"success": True})
  1145→    except Exception as err:  # noqa: BLE001 - WS handler must catch all to send error response
  1146→        connection.send_error(
  1147→            msg["id"],
  1148→            websocket_api.const.ERR_UNKNOWN_ERROR,
  1149→            str(err),
  1150→        )
  1151→
  1152→
  1153→# Supported domains for condition entities
  1154→CONDITION_ENTITY_DOMAINS = frozenset(
  1155→    {
  1156→        BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN,
  1157→        CALENDAR_DOMAIN,
  1158→        INPUT_BOOLEAN_DOMAIN,
  1159→        SCHEDULE_DOMAIN,
  1160→        SWITCH_DOMAIN,
  1161→    }
  1162→)
  1163→
  1164→
  1165→@websocket_api.websocket_command(
  1166→    {
  1167→        vol.Required("type"): "lock_code_manager/update_slot_condition",
  1168→        vol.Exclusive("config_entry_title", "entry"): str,
  1169→        vol.Exclusive("config_entry_id", "entry"): str,
  1170→        vol.Required(ATTR_SLOT): int,
  1171→        vol.Optional(CONF_ENTITY_ID): vol.Any(
  1172→            cv.entity_domain(CONDITION_ENTITY_DOMAINS), None
  1173→        ),
  1174→        vol.Optional(CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES): vol.Any(
  1175→            vol.All(vol.Coerce(int), vol.Range(min=1)), None
  1176→        ),
  1177→    }
  1178→)
  1179→@websocket_api.async_response
  1180→@async_get_entry
  1181→async def update_slot_condition(
  1182→    hass: HomeAssistant,
  1183→    connection: websocket_api.ActiveConnection,
  1184→    msg: dict[str, Any],
  1185→    config_entry: ConfigEntry,
  1186→) -> None:
  1187→    """
  1188→    Update condition settings for a slot.
  1189→
  1190→    Allows adding, changing, or removing:
  1191→    - entity_id: Condition entity (calendar, schedule, binary_sensor, switch, input_boolean)
  1192→    - number_of_uses: Usage tracking (positive int to enable, None to disable)
  1193→
  1194→    Only fields present in the message are updated. To remove a field, pass None/null.
  1195→    """
  1196→    slot_num = msg[ATTR_SLOT]
  1197→
  1198→    # Validate slot exists
  1199→    slots = get_entry_data(config_entry, CONF_SLOTS, {})
  1200→    slot_key = slot_num if slot_num in slots else str(slot_num)
  1201→    if slot_key not in slots:
  1202→        connection.send_error(
  1203→            msg["id"],
  1204→            websocket_api.const.ERR_NOT_FOUND,
  1205→            f"Slot {slot_num} not found in config entry",
  1206→        )
  1207→        return
  1208→
  1209→    # Verify entity exists if provided
  1210→    if (entity_id := msg.get(CONF_ENTITY_ID)) is not None and not hass.states.get(
  1211→        entity_id
  1212→    ):
  1213→        connection.send_error(
  1214→            msg["id"],
  1215→            websocket_api.const.ERR_NOT_FOUND,
  1216→            f"Entity {entity_id} not found",
  1217→        )
  1218→        return
  1219→
  1220→    # Check for excluded platforms with a single registry lookup
  1221→    if entity_id is not None:
  1222→        ent_reg = er.async_get(hass)
  1223→        entity_entry = ent_reg.async_get(entity_id)
  1224→        if entity_entry and entity_entry.platform in EXCLUDED_CONDITION_PLATFORMS:
  1225→            connection.send_error(
  1226→                msg["id"],
  1227→                websocket_api.const.ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED,
  1228→                f"Entities from the '{entity_entry.platform}' integration are not "
  1229→                "supported as condition entities. See the wiki for details: "
  1230→                "https://github.com/raman325/lock_code_manager/wiki/"
  1231→                "Unsupported-Condition-Entity-Integrations",
  1232→            )
  1233→            return
  1234→
  1235→    # Update config entry data
  1236→    data = copy.deepcopy(dict(config_entry.data))
  1237→    slot_config = data[CONF_SLOTS][slot_key]
  1238→
  1239→    # Update entity_id if present in message
  1240→    if CONF_ENTITY_ID in msg:
  1241→        entity_id = msg[CONF_ENTITY_ID]
  1242→        if entity_id is None:
  1243→            # Remove the key entirely
  1244→            slot_config.pop(CONF_ENTITY_ID, None)
  1245→        else:
  1246→            slot_config[CONF_ENTITY_ID] = entity_id
  1247→
  1248→    # Update number_of_uses if present in message
  1249→    if CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES in msg:
  1250→        num_uses = msg[CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES]
  1251→        if num_uses is None:
  1252→            # Remove the key entirely (disables tracking, removes entity)
  1253→            slot_config.pop(CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES, None)
  1254→        else:
  1255→            slot_config[CONF_NUMBER_OF_USES] = num_uses
  1256→
  1257→    data[CONF_SLOTS][slot_key] = slot_config
  1258→
  1259→    # Only update if data actually changed
  1260→    if data != config_entry.data:
  1261→        # Set options to trigger async_update_listener which will:
  1262→        # 1. Create/remove entities as needed
  1263→        # 2. Copy options to data and clear options
  1264→        # We must NOT update data directly here, as the listener compares
  1265→        # config_entry.data (old) with config_entry.options (new) to detect changes
  1266→        hass.config_entries.async_update_entry(config_entry, options=data)
  1267→
  1268→    connection.send_result(msg["id"], {"success": True})

### 3. **What does the duplicate code event handler from PR #899 do?**

**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 478-540)

`_async_handle_duplicate_code()` flow:

1. **Event Detection** (lines 451-462): When Z-Wave event `NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE` (AccessControl notification event 15) arrives:
   - Line 453: Only triggers if `self._set_in_progress_code_slot` is not None (LCM initiated the set)
   - Line 454: Checks if the event's userId matches 0 or the in-progress slot
   - Lines 458-462: Creates async task to handle it

2. **Handler Actions** (lines 479-540):
   - Line 482: Clears the `_set_in_progress_code_slot` tracking
   - Lines 486-498: Finds the config entry managing this lock/slot combination
   - Lines 501-508: Gets the "enabled" switch entity ID for this slot
   - Lines 510-516: **Turns off the switch** (`SERVICE_TURN_OFF`) to disable the slot (line 511)
   - Lines 518-540: Creates a persistent notification to the user with message:
     ```
     "Lock {lock_entity_id} rejected the code for slot {code_slot} (config entry {title}) 
      because it duplicates a code in another slot. Slot {code_slot} has been disabled. 
      Please set a unique code and re-enable slot {code_slot}"
     ```

---

### 4. **Is there any existing duplicate code detection before sending to the lock?**

**No, there is NO pre-flight duplicate code detection.** The system is reactive, not preventive:

1. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py` (lines 594-620)
   - `async_set_usercode()` only checks if the code is **already set to the same value** (lines 603-609) to avoid unnecessary network calls
   - No validation of whether the code duplicates another slot's code

2. The system relies on:
   - The **lock's native duplicate detection** (which sends the Z-Wave notification)
   - The **event handler (PR #899)** to react to that notification
   - User re-enabling and setting a unique code

3. **File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/binary_sensor.py` (lines 587-608)

- **Direct path** (user calls `text.set_value` or websocket): Raise an error / fail the action. The user set the PIN explicitly, so they should get immediate feedback, not have their slot silently disabled.

**Implementation:** `async_set_usercode` needs to know which path called it. Options:
- **(A)** Add a parameter like `disable_on_duplicate: bool = True` — sync passes `True`, direct sets pass `False`
- **(B)** Raise a specific exception (`DuplicateCodeError`) and let the caller decide — sync catches it and disables, direct path lets it propagate

I'd recommend **(B)** — it keeps `async_set_usercode` focused on detection/raising, and pushes the policy (disable vs fail) to the callers who know the context.

**Masked slots fallback:** Event 15 handler stays as safety net.

- Which slots LCM sees vs manages
- How data flows between coordinator, provider, and sync sensor

## Design

### 1. Pre-flight Duplicate Detection

**Where:** `async_set_usercode()` in `zwave_js.py`, before the Z-Wave service call.

**Logic:** Scan `self.coordinator.data` for any other slot with the same PIN value.
Skip masked values (all `*`). Skip the target slot.

**New exception:** `DuplicateCodeError` — raised by `async_set_usercode()` when a
duplicate is found. Includes the conflicting slot number and whether it's managed or
unmanaged.

**Caller-specific handling:**

- **Sync path** (`_perform_sync_operation`): Catches `DuplicateCodeError`, disables
  the slot via the enabled switch, creates a persistent notification identifying the
  conflicting slot.
- **Direct path** (`text.set_value`, websocket): Lets the exception propagate — the
  action fails with a clear error message. The user explicitly set the PIN, so they
  should get immediate feedback, not have their slot silently disabled.

**Masked slots:** For unmanaged slots with masked PINs, we can't know the actual
code. The existing event 15 handler stays as a safety net for these cases. The two
approaches are complementary.

### 2. Config Flow Text Updates

**`choose_path` step description:**

> "Some locks mask stored PINs, making them invisible to external tools. Once LCM is
> managing your locks, do not set codes using other tools or manually at the lock.
> Clear all slots before setup. For permanent access codes, configure them through LCM
> without a condition entity."

**`num_slots` step description:**

> "Configure slots for all codes you want on the lock (existing user codes for the
> slot range specified will be cleared). You can add and remove slots later using the
> integration services."

### 3. Architecture Documentation

**New file:** `ARCHITECTURE.md` in the repo root.

Covers the system-level data flow:
- What gets polled vs pushed vs hard-refreshed
- Coordinator data model (all slots, managed + unmanaged)
- Sync decision flow (binary sensor)
- Managed vs unmanaged slot distinction
- Push update processing
- Masked PIN resolution
- Rate limiting and serialization

**Module docstrings:** Upgrade from one-liners to 2-3 sentences describing the
module's role + pointer to `ARCHITECTURE.md`. Files: `coordinator.py`,
`binary_sensor.py`, `zwave_js.py`, `_base.py`.

### 4. Event

# Duplicate Code Detection Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** Prevent infinite sync loops caused by duplicate PINs across lock slots by detecting conflicts before sending codes to the lock.

**Architecture:** Add `DuplicateCodeError` exception. Pre-flight check in `async_set_usercode()` scans coordinator data for matching PINs (skipping masked values). class DuplicateCodeError(LockCodeManagerError):
    """Raised when a PIN duplicates a code in another slot on the lock."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        code_slot: int,
        conflicting_slot: int,
        conflicting_slot_managed: bool,
        lock_entity_id: str,
    ):
        """Initialize the error."""
        self.code_slot = code_slot
        self.conflicting_slot = conflicting_slot
        self.conflicting_slot_managed = conflicting_slot_managed
        self.lock_entity_id = lock_entity_id
        managed_str = "managed" if conflicting_slot_managed else "unmanaged"
        super().__init__(
            f"Lock {lock_entity_id}: cannot set code on slot {code_slot} — "
            f"PIN duplicates {managed_str} slot {conflicting_slot}"
        )
```

**Step 3: Write the implementation**

In `binary_sensor.py`, modify `_perform_sync_operation` (line ~502). Add a catch for `DuplicateCodeError` alongside the existing `LockDisconnected` catch:

```python
    async def _perform_sync_operation(self, slot_state: _SlotState) -> bool:
        """
        Perform sync operation (set or clear usercode).

        Returns True if sync was performed, False if lock disconnected.
        Raises nothing on duplicate — disables slot and notifies instead.
        """
        try:
            if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
                await self.lock.async_internal_set_usercode(
                    int(self.slot_num), slot_state.pin_state, slot_state.name_state
                )
                _LOGGER.debug(
                    "%s (%s): Set usercode for %s slot %s",
                    self.config_entry.entry_id,
                    self.config_entry.title,
                    self.lock.lock.entity_id,
                    self.slot_num,
                )
            else:
                await self.lock.async_internal_clear_usercode(int(self.slot_num))
                _LOGGER.debug(
                    "%s (%s): Cleared usercode for %s slot %s",
                    self.config_entry.entry_id,
                    self.config_entry.title,
                    self.lock.lock.entity_id,
                    self.slot_num,
                )
            self._cancel_retry()
            return True
        except DuplicateCodeError as err:
            _LOGGER.error("%s", err)
            # Disable the slot — this is a permanent error, not transient
            await self.hass.services.async_call(
                SWITCH_DOMAIN,
                SERVICE_TURN_OFF,
                {ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self._enabled_entity_id},
                blocking=True,
            )
            managed_str = "managed" if err.conflicting_slot_managed else "unmanaged"
            async_create(
                self.hass,
                (
                    f"Lock {err.lock_entity_id}: cannot set code on slot "
                    f"{err.code_slot} because the same PIN is already in "
                    f"{managed_str} slot {err.conflicting_slot}. "
                    f"Slot {err.code_slot} has been disabled. Set a unique "
                    f"code and re-enable the slot."
                ),
                title="Duplicate Lock Code",
                notification_id=(
                    f"{DOMAIN}_{err.lock_entity_id}_{err.code_slot}_dupe"
                ),
            )
            self._cancel_retry()
            return True  # Operation "handled" — don't retry
        except LockDisconnected as err:
            _LOGGER.debug(
                "%s (%s): Unable to %s usercode for %s slot %s: %s",
                self.config_entry.entry_id,
                self.config_entry.title,
                "set" if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON else "clear",
                self.lock.lock.entity_id,
                self.slot_num,
                err,
            )
            self._schedule_retry()
            return False
```

### Task 6: Architecture Documentation

**Step 1: Create ARCHITECTURE.md**

Write `ARCHITECTURE.md` covering:

1. **Overview** — LCM manages usercodes across locks via providers. Each lock gets a coordinator that holds the full slot→code mapping (managed AND unmanaged).

2. **Data Flow** diagram (text):
   ```
   Config Entry (desired state)
       ↓
   Binary Sensor (sync decision)
       ↓ set/clear
   Provider (async_set_usercode / async_clear_usercode)
       ↓ Z-Wave service call
   Lock (firmware)
       ↓ push notification / poll response
   Coordinator (actual state: ALL slots)
       ↓ listener notification
   Binary Sensor (re-evaluate sync)
   ```

3. **Coordinator** — Stores `dict[int, int | str]` mapping slot number to code for ALL slots on the lock. Does not distinguish managed vs unmanaged. Push-based providers set `update_interval = None`; poll-based providers use periodic refresh.

4. **Push vs Poll vs Hard Refresh**:
   - **Push**: Z-Wave value update events → provider filters → `coordinator.push_update()`
   - **Poll**: Coordinator's `_async_update_data()` → provider's `async_get_usercodes()` on interval
   - **Hard refresh**: `async_hard_refresh_codes()` → refreshes Z-Wave CC values cache from device, then reads all slots

5. **Managed vs Unmanaged Slots** — A slot is "managed" if it exists in any LCM config entry's `CONF_SLOTS` for this lock AND the corresponding LCM entities exist. Detection: `_get_slot_entity_states()` returns `None` for unmanaged. Coordinator stores both; sync only operates on managed; UI displays both.

6. **Sync Decision** — Binary sensor compares desired state (PIN from text entity, active from condition) against actual state (coordinator data). If active + PIN differs → set. If inactive + code present → clear.

7. **Masked PINs** — Some locks return `****` instead of actual codes. For managed slots, resolved via the text entity's expected PIN. For unmanaged slots, kept as-is (just needs non-empty to indicate "in use").

8. **Duplicate Code Detection** — Pre-flight check in `async_set_usercode()` scans coordinator data. Event 15 handler as safety net for masked/unknown slots.

9. **Rate Limiting** — All lock operations serialized via `asyncio.Lock` per provider instance, with 2-second minimum delay between operations.

- `coordinator.py`: "Manages the slot→code mapping for a single lock. Stores ALL slots (managed and unmanaged). See ARCHITECTURE.md for the full data flow."
- `binary_sensor.py`: "Sync sensor that compares desired state (config entries) against actual state (coordinator data) and triggers set/clear operations to reconcile. See ARCHITECTURE.md for the sync decision flow."
- `providers/zwave_js.py`: "Z-Wave JS lock provider. Handles push updates, masked PIN resolution, duplicate code detection, and rate-limited set/clear operations. See ARCHITECTURE.md for the provider's role in the data flow."
- `providers/_base.py`: "Abstract base for lock providers. Handles rate limiting, connection checking, and coordinator refresh after operations. See ARCHITECTURE.md for the provider interface contract."

**`push_update` Method (Lines 64-76):**
```python
@callback
def push_update(self, updates: dict[int, int | str]) -> None:
    """Push one or more slot updates and notify listening entities."""
    if not updates:
        return
    
    new_data = {**self.data, **updates}
    # Skip update if data hasn't actually changed to avoid redundant logging
    # and unnecessary listener notifications
    if new_data == self.data:
        return
    
    self.async_set_updated_data(new_data)
```

    35→**`push_update` Method (Lines 64-76):**

    37→@callback
    38→def push_update(self, updates: dict[int, int | str]) -> None:
    39→    """Push one or more slot updates and notify listening entities."""
    40→    if not updates:
    41→        return
    42→    
    43→    new_data = {**self.data, **updates}
    44→    # Skip update if data hasn't actually changed to avoid redundant logging
    45→    # and unnecessary listener notifications
    46→    if new_data == self.data:
    47→        return
    48→    
    49→    self.async_set_updated_data(new_data)

    51→- **Called directly by Z-Wave JS provider** for optimistic updates

    72→### 2. SYNC FLOW - BINARY SENSOR
    73→
    74→**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/custom_components/lock_code_manager/binary_sensor.py`
    75→
    76→**Module Docstring (Line 1):**

    79→```
    80→
    81→**Entity: `LockCodeManagerCodeSlotInSyncEntity`** (Lines 248-612)
    82→
    83→**`_SlotState` Dataclass (Lines 439-445):**
    84→```python
    85→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    86→class _SlotState:
    87→    active_state: str              # STATE_ON or STATE_OFF
    88→    pin_state: str                 # Expected PIN from text entity
    89→    name_state: str | None         # Name for the code (optional)
    90→    code_state: str                # Current code on lock (from sensor entity)
    91→    coordinator_code: int | str | None  # Code from coordinator data
    92→```
    93→
    94→**`_calculate_expected_sync` Decision Logic (Lines 422-437):**
    95→```python
    96→def _calculate_expected_sync(self, slot_state: _SlotState) -> bool:
    97→    """
    98→    Calculate whether slot should be in sync.
    99→    
   100→    Active: PIN should match code on lock
   101→    Inactive: Code on lock should be empty
   102→    """
   103→    # Use coordinator data if available, otherwise fall back to sensor state
   104→    lock_code = (
   105→        str(slot_state.coordinator_code)
   106→        if slot_state.coordinator_code is not None
   107→        else slot_state.code_state
   108→    )
   109→    if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
   110→        return slot_state.pin_state == lock_code
   111→    return lock_code == ""
   112→```
   113→
   114→**Sync Decision Logic:**
   115→1. **If slot ACTIVE (STATE_ON):** Expected PIN must match actual code on lock
   116→2. **If slot INACTIVE (STATE_OFF):** Code on lock must be cleared (empty string)
   117→3. **Data source priority:** Coordinator data > sensor state
   118→
   119→**`_perform_sync_operation` (Lines 501-541):**
   120→```python
   121→async def _perform_sync_operation(self, slot_state: _SlotState) -> bool:
   122→    """
   123→    Perform sync operation (set or clear usercode).
   124→    
   125→    Returns True if sync was performed, False if lock disconnected.
   126→    """
   127→    try:
   128→        if slot_state.active_state == STATE_ON:
   129→            await self.lock.async_internal_set_usercode(
   130→                int(self.slot_num), slot_state.pin_state, slot_state.name_state
   131→            )
   132→            _LOGGER.debug(...)
   133→        else:  # active_state == STATE_OFF
   134→            await self.lock.async_internal_clear_usercode(int(self.slot_num))
   135→            _LOGGER.debug(...)
   136→        self._cancel_retry()
   137→        return True
   138→    except LockDisconnected as err:
   139→        _LOGGER.debug(...)
   140→        self._schedule_retry()
   141→        return False
   142→```
   143→
   144→**When Sync Operations Occur (Lines 543-612 `_async_update_state`):**
   145→1. **Initial Load** (`_attr_is_on is None`):
   146→   - Sets sync state WITHOUT performing operations
   147→   - Prevents startup flapping
   148→   - Returns early (no operations)
   149→
   150→2. **Normal Operation** (after initial load):
   151→   - **If out of sync** (`expected_in_sync == False`):
   152→     - Sets `is_on = False`
   153→     - Performs sync operation (set or clear)
   154→     - Refreshes coordinator to verify
   155→     - Schedules retry on failure
   156→   - **If now in sync** (was out, now aligned):
   157→     - Sets `is_on = True`
   158→     - Cancels any pending retries
   159→
   160→**Entry Point (Lines 291-310 `async_update`):**
   161→```python
   162→async def async_update(self) -> None:
   163→    """Update entity."""
   164→    if self._attr_is_on is None:
   165→        return
   166→    
   167→    if (
   168→        self._lock.locked()
   169→        or self.is_on
   170→        or not (state := self.hass.states.get(self.lock.lock.entity_id))
   171→        or state.state in (STATE_UNAVAILABLE, STATE_UNKNOWN)
   172→        or (not self.coordinator.last_update_success and not self._retry_active)
   173→    ):
   174→        return
   175→    
   176→    _LOGGER.debug(...)
   177→    await self._async_update_state()
   178→```
   179→**Sync only occurs if:**
   180→- Slot is NOT already in sync (`self.is_on == False`)
   181→- Lock is NOT physically locked
   182→- Lock entity is available
   183→- Coordinator has successful updates (or retry is active)

   216→
   217→**Push Update Processing (full flow in Lines 291-389):**
   218→
   219→1. **Filter Step:**
   220→   - Only handle `CommandClass.USER_CODE`
   221→   - Only handle `userCode` or `userIdStatus` properties
   222→   - Skip slot 0 (metadata, not a user code slot)
   223→
   224→2. **Clear In-Progress Tracking:**

   232→     - If NO → Update coordinator with empty string `""`
   233→   - Exits early (doesn't process as code update)

   235→4. **Handle Code Updates** (`userCode` property):
   236→   - Normalizes `None`, empty, or all-zeros to `""`
   237→   - Resolves masked codes (all asterisks) via `_resolve_pin_if_masked`
   238→   - Skips duplicate events (compares with existing coordinator data)
   239→   - **Calls:** `self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: resolved})`
   240→
   241→**Does NOT distinguish managed vs unmanaged slots in push handling** — processes ALL slots but may skip based on state expectations.

   245→### 4. MASKED PIN RESOLUTION
   246→
   247→**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py`
   248→
   249→**`_resolve_pin_if_masked` Method (Lines 229-271):**
   250→```python
   251→def _resolve_pin_if_masked(self, value: str, code_slot: int) -> str | None:
   252→    """
   253→    Resolve a PIN value, looking up expected PIN if masked.
   254→    
   255→    Some locks return masked values (all asterisks) instead of the actual PIN.
   256→    This method returns the value as-is if not masked, or looks up the expected
   257→    PIN from LCM entities if masked.
   258→    """
   259→    slot_in_use = self.code_slot_in_use(code_slot)
   260→    
   261→    if value == "0" * len(value) and slot_in_use is False:
   262→        # Some locks return all zeros instead of a blank value when cleared - treat
   263→        # as unmasked cleared value
   264→        return ""
   265→    
   266→    # If not masked, return as-is
   267→    if not value or not (value == "*" * len(value) and slot_in_use):
   268→        return value
   269→    
   270→    # Masked - look up expected PIN from LCM entities
   271→    # Use ATTR_ACTIVE binary sensor (not CONF_ENABLED switch) to match what
   272→    # sync logic expects. ATTR_ACTIVE considers conditions, so we only resolve
   273→    # masked codes when sync expects a PIN on the lock.
   274→    states = self._get_slot_entity_states(code_slot)
   275→    if states is None:
   276→        return None
   277→    
   278→    active_state, pin_value, pin_entity_id = states
   279→    
   280→    if active_state == STATE_ON:
   281→        _LOGGER.debug(
   282→            "PIN is masked for lock %s code slot %s, assuming value from PIN entity %s",
   283→            self.lock.entity_id,
   284→            code_slot,
   285→            pin_entity_id,
   286→        )
   287→        return pin_value
   288→    
   289→    # Fall back to returning masked value if active state is not ON (e.g. slot not
   290→    # enabled) - we don't care what the value is, just that there is one so that
   291→    # the sync logic treats this slot as having a PIN set on the lock
   292→    return value
   293→```
   294→
   295→**Key aspects:**
   296→- Detects masks: all asterisks with `slot_in_use == True`
   297→- Looks up expected PIN from LCM text entity (for MANAGED slots only)
   298→- Returns `None` if slot is managed but PIN entity doesn't exist (unresolvable)
   299→- Falls back to returning masked value for unmanaged slots (just needs non-empty)
   300→
   301→**Helper Method (Lines 179-213 `_get_slot_entity_states`):**
   302→```python
   303→def _get_slot_entity_states(self, code_slot: int) -> tuple[str, str, str] | None:
   304→    """
   305→    Get active state, PIN value, and PIN entity ID for a managed slot.
   306→    
   307→    Returns tuple of (active_state, pin_value, pin_entity_id) if the slot is
   308→    managed by LCM and entities exist, None otherwise.
   309→    """
   310→    try:
   311→        config_entry = next(
   312→            entry
   313→            for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
   314→            if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
   315→            and code_slot in (int(s) for s in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {}))
   316→        )
   317→    except StopIteration:
   318→        return None  # Not managed by any LCM entry
   319→    
   320→    base_unique_id = f"{config_entry.entry_id}|{code_slot}"
   321→    active_entity_id = self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
   322→        BINARY_SENSOR_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f"{base_unique_id}|{ATTR_ACTIVE}"
   323→    )
   324→    pin_entity_id = self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
   325→        TEXT_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f"{base_unique_id}|{CONF_PIN}"
   326→    )
   327→    
   328→    if not active_entity_id or not pin_entity_id:
   329→        return None  # Entities don't exist yet
   330→    
   331→    active_state = self.hass.states.get(active_entity_id)
   332→    pin_state = self.hass.states.get(pin_entity_id)
   333→    
   334→    if not active_state or not pin_state:
   335→        return None
   336→    
   337→    return (active_state.state, pin_state.state, pin_entity_id)
   338→```
   339→
   340→**This IS how managed vs unmanaged is distinguished:**
   341→- **MANAGED:** Slot exists in a config entry's `CONF_SLOTS`, and corresponding LCM entities exist
   342→- **UNMANAGED:** Slot either not in any config entry, or entities don't exist yet

   348→**File:** `/Users/raman/projects/lock_code_manager/providers/zwave_js.py`

   350→**Entry Point (Lines 438-476 `_handle_zwave_js_event`):**

   352→@callback

   362→    # Handle duplicate code rejection — only when LCM initiated the set

   366→        and self._set_in_progress_code_slot is not None
   367→        and code_slot in (0, self._set_in_progress_code_slot)
   368→    ):
   369→        slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot or code_slot
   370→        self.hass.async_create_task(

   379→**Handler Execution (Lines 478-539 `_async_handle_duplicate_code`):**
   380→```python
   381→async def _async_handle_duplicate_code(self) -> None:
   382→    """Handle duplicate code rejection from the lock."""
   383→    lock_ent_id = self.lock.entity_id
   384→    code_slot = self._set_in_progress_code_slot
   385→    self._set_in_progress_code_slot = None
   386→    if code_slot is None:
   387→        return
   388→    try:
   389→        # Only move forward if a config entry manages this lock and code slot
   390→        entry = next(
   391→            entry
   392→            for entry in self.hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
   393→            if self.lock.entity_id in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_LOCKS, [])
   394→            and code_slot in (int(s) for s in get_entry_data(entry, CONF_SLOTS, {}))
   395→        )
   396→    except StopIteration:
   397→        return  # Not managed - ignore
   398→    
   399→    # Guard that will never trigger but satisfies type checkers
   400→    if (

400→    if (
   401→        enabled_ent_id := self.ent_reg.async_get_entity_id(
   402→            SWITCH_DOMAIN, DOMAIN, f"{entry.entry_id}|{code_slot}|{CONF_ENABLED}"
   403→        )
   404→    ) is None:
   405→        return
   406→    
   407→    # DISABLE the slot
   408→    await self.hass.services.async_call(
   409→        SWITCH_DOMAIN,
   410→        SERVICE_TURN_OFF,
   411→        {ATTR_ENTITY_ID: enabled_ent_id},
   412→        blocking=True,
   413→    )
   414→    
   415→    # Create user notification
   416→    entry_title = entry.title or entry.entry_id
   417→    def _dupe_msg(...) -> str:
   418→        return (
   419→            f"Lock {lock_entity_id} rejected the code for slot {code_slot} "
   420→            f"(config entry {config_entry_title}) because it duplicates a code "
   421→            f"in another slot. Slot {code_slot} has been disabled. Please set a "
   422→            f"unique code and re-enable slot {code_slot}"
   423→        )
   424→    
   425→    _LOGGER.error(_dupe_msg(...))
   426→    async_create(
   427→        self.hass,
   428→        _dupe_msg(...),
   429→        title="Duplicate Lock Code Rejected",
   430→        notification_id=(f"{DOMAIN}_{lock_ent_id}_{code_slot}_dupe"),
   431→    )
   432→```
   433→
   434→**Complete reactive flow:**
   435→1. Z-Wave JS event listener detects `NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE` notification
   436→2. Verifies it matches `_set_in_progress_code_slot` (LCM initiated the set)
   437→3. Checks if slot is MANAGED by a config entry
   438→4. If managed: Disables the slot via switch entity and notifies user
   439→5. If unmanaged: Silently ignores (lock rejected duplicate, but LCM didn't manage it)

   443→### 6. SET/CLEAR OPERATIONS WITH OPTIMISTIC UPDATES
   444→
   445→**Z-Wave JS Provider (Lines 579-627 `async_set_usercode`):**
   446→```python
   447→async def async_set_usercode(
   448→    self, code_slot: int, usercode: int | str, name: str | None = None
   449→) -> bool:
   450→    """
   451→    Set a usercode on a code slot.
   452→    
   453→    Returns True if the value was changed, False if already set to this value.
   454→    """
   455→    usercode = str(usercode)
   456→    try:
   457→        if (current := get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)).get("in_use"):
   458→            current_code = str(current.get("usercode", ""))
   459→            # Resolve masked codes and compare
   460→            if usercode == self._resolve_pin_if_masked(current_code, code_slot):
   461→                _LOGGER.debug(...)
   462→                return False  # Already set to this value
   463→    except Exception:
   464→        pass  # Proceed with the set
   465→    
   466→    self._set_in_progress_code_slot = code_slot
   467→    service_data = {
   468→        ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
   469→        ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
   470→        ATTR_USERCODE: usercode,
   471→    }
   472→    await self.async_call_service(
   473→        ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_SET_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
   474→    )
   475→    # V1 locks don't reliably update cache after set - poll directly
   476→    if self._usercode_cc_version < 2:
   477→        await get_usercode_from_node(self.node, code_slot)
   478→    # Optimistic update: command succeeded, update coordinator immediately
   479→    if self.coordinator:
   480→        self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: usercode})
   481→    return True
   482→```
   483→
   484→**Clear Operation (Lines 629-668 `async_clear_usercode`):**
   485→```python
   486→async def async_clear_usercode(self, code_slot: int) -> bool:
   487→    """
   488→    Clear a usercode on a code slot.
   489→    
   490→    Returns True if the value was changed, False if already cleared.
   491→    """
   492→    try:
   493→        current = get_usercode(self.node, code_slot)
   494→        if not current.get("in_use"):
   495→            _LOGGER.debug(...)
   496→            return False  # Already cleared
   497→    except Exception:
   498→        pass
   499→    
   500→    service_data = {
   501→        ATTR_ENTITY_ID: self.lock.entity_id,
   502→        ATTR_CODE_SLOT: code_slot,
   503→    }
   504→    await self.async_call_service(
   505→        ZWAVE_JS_DOMAIN, SERVICE_CLEAR_LOCK_USERCODE, service_data
   506→    )
   507→    # V1 locks need direct poll for cache update
   508→    if self._usercode_cc_version < 2:
   509→        await get_usercode_from_node(self.node, code_slot)
   510→    # Optimistic update
   511→    if self.coordinator:
   512→        self.coordinator.push_update({code_slot: ""})
   513→    return True
   514→```
   515→
   516→**Key flow:**
   517→1. Check cache if code is already set/cleared (skip if no change)
   518→2. Track slot in `_set_in_progress_code_slot` (for duplicate detection)
   519→3. Call Z-Wave service
   520→4. V1 locks: Force cache poll from device
   521→5. Z-Wave JS: Optimistically update coordinator via `push_update`
   522→   - Push-based providers do this to avoid sync loops from stale cache
   523→   - Non-push providers refresh coordinator after, reading from cache
   524→
   525→**Base Wrapper (Lines 534-548 `async_internal_set_usercode`):**
   526→```python
   527→@final
   528→async def async_internal_set_usercode(
   529→    self, code_slot: int, usercode: int | str, name: str | None = None
   530→) -> None:
   531→    """Set a usercode on a code slot."""
   532→    changed = await self._execute_rate_limited(
   533→        "set", self.async_set_usercode, code_slot, usercode, name=name
   534→    )
   535→    # Refresh coordinator to update entity states from cache (only if changed).
   536→    # Skip for push-based providers — they update the coordinator optimistically
   537→    # via push_update() in their set/clear methods, and refreshing from cache
   538→    # could overwrite the optimistic update with stale data when the underlying
   539→    # driver defers cache updates until device confirmation.
   540→    if changed and self.coordinator and not self.supports_push:
   541→        await self.coordinator.async_request_refresh()
   542→```

   545→
   546→### 7. RATE LIMITING & SERIALIZATION
   547→
   548→**Base Provider (Lines 139-172 `_execute_rate_limited`):**
   549→```python
   550→async def _execute_rate_limited(
   551→    self,
   552→    operation_type: Literal["get", "set", "clear", "refresh"],
   553→    func: Callable[..., Awaitable[Any]],
   554→    *args: Any,
   555→    **kwargs: Any,
   556→) -> Any:
   557→    """Execute operation with connection check, serialization, and delay."""
   558→    if not await self.async_internal_is_connection_up():
   559→        raise LockDisconnected(
   560→            f"Cannot {_OPERATION_MESSAGES[operation_type]} {self.lock.entity_id} - lock not connected"
   561→        )
   562→    
   563→    async with self._aio_lock:  # Serializes ALL lock operations
   564→        elapsed = time.monotonic() - self._last_operation_time
   565→        if elapsed < self._min_operation_delay:
   566→            delay = self._min_operation_delay - elapsed
   567→            LOGGER.debug(...)
   568→            await asyncio.sleep(delay)
   569→        
   570→        LOGGER.debug(...)
   571→        result = await func(*args, **kwargs)
   572→        self._last_operation_time = time.monotonic()
   573→        return result
   574→```
   575→
   576→**Rate Limiting Constants:** 2-second minimum delay between lock operations (defined as `MIN_OPERATION_DELAY` in _base.py)

   597→        return

   622→
   623→### 9. MANAGED VS UNMANAGED DISTINCTION
   624→
   625→**KEY FINDING:** The codebase does NOT distinguish managed vs unmanaged at the coordinator level. The distinction is made at the PUSH UPDATE and MASKED PIN RESOLUTION level:
   626→
   627→**Managed Slot:** 
   628→- Exists in a config entry's `CONF_SLOTS` dictionary
   629→- Has corresponding LCM binary sensor (ATTR_ACTIVE) and text entity (CONF_PIN)
   630→- When masked PIN is received, it's resolved to expected PIN from text entity
   631→- When duplicate code notification occurs, slot is disabled via switch entity
   632→
   633→**Unmanaged Slot:**
   634→- Does not exist in any LCM config entry's slots
   635→- Or exists but entities haven't been created yet
   636→- Masked values are kept as-is (or return `None`)
   637→- Duplicate code notifications are silently ignored

   640→- Searches all LCM config entries for one managing this lock and slot
   641→- Returns `None` if not found or entities don't exist → unmanaged

   676→## Section 1 (revised): Pre-flight Duplicate Detection

   729→# Duplicate Code Detection + Architecture Documentation
   730→
   731→**Date:** 2026-03-28
   732→**Status:** Approved
   733→
   734→## Problem
   735→
   736→Some locks reject setting a PIN if the same code exists in another slot. When this
   737→happens, the Z-Wave service call succeeds but the lock silently rejects it. LCM sees
   738→the code isn't set, retries, and loops indefinitely.
   739→
   740→PR #899 added reactive handling via Z-Wave event 15
   741→(`NEW_USER_CODE_NOT_ADDED_DUE_TO_DUPLICATE_CODE`), but users still report infinite
   742→sync loops — likely because not all locks emit this event reliably.
   743→
   744→Additionally, the codebase lacks clarity on:
   745→- Which slots LCM sees vs manages
   746→- How data flows between coordinator, provider, and sync sensor
   747→- The managed vs unmanaged distinction (implicit, scattered across modules)
   748→
   749→## Design
   750→
   751→### 1. Pre-flight Duplicate Detection
   752→
   753→**Where:** `async_set_usercode()` in `zwave_js.py`, before the Z-Wave service call.
   754→
   755→**Logic:** Scan `self.coordinator.data` for any other slot with the same PIN value.
   756→Skip masked values (all `*`). Skip the target slot.
   757→
   758→**New exception:** `DuplicateCodeError` — raised by `async_set_usercode()` when a
   759→duplicate is found. Includes the conflicting slot number and whether it's managed or
   760→unmanaged.
   761→
   762→**Caller-specific handling:**
   763→
   764→- **Sync path** (`_perform_sync_operation`): Catches `DuplicateCodeError`, disables
   765→  the slot via the enabled switch, creates a persistent notification identifying the
   766→  conflicting slot.
   767→- **Direct path** (`text.set_value`, websocket): Lets the exception propagate — the
   768→  action fails with a clear error message. The user explicitly set the PIN, so they
   769→  should get immediate feedback, not have their slot silently disabled.
   770→
   771→**Masked slots:** For unmanaged slots with masked PINs, we can't know the actual
   772→code. The existing event 15 handler stays as a safety net for these cases. The two
   773→approaches are complementary.
   774→
   775→### 2. Config Flow Text Updates
   776→
   777→**`choose_path` step description:**
   778→
   779→> "Some locks mask stored PINs, making them invisible to external tools. Once LCM is
   780→> managing your locks, do not set codes using other tools or manually at the lock.
   781→> Clear all slots before setup. For permanent access codes, configure them through LCM
   782→> without a condition entity."
   783→
   784→**`num_slots` step description:**
   785→
   786→> "Configure slots for all codes you want on the lock (existing user codes for the
   787→> slot range specified will be cleared). You can add and remove slots later using the
   788→> integration services."
   789→
   790→### 3. Architecture Documentation
   791→
   792→**New file:** `ARCHITECTURE.md` in the repo root.
   793→
   794→Covers the system-level data flow:
   795→- What gets polled vs pushed vs hard-refreshed
   796→- Coordinator data model (all slots, managed + unmanaged)
   797→- Sync decision flow (binary sensor)
   798→- Managed vs unmanaged slot distinction
   799→- Push update processing
   800→- Masked PIN resolution
   801→- Rate limiting and serialization
   802→
   803→**Module docstrings:** Upgrade from one-liners to 2-3 sentences describing the
   804→module's role + pointer to `ARCHITECTURE.md`. Files: `coordinator.py`,
   805→`binary_sensor.py`, `zwave_js.py`, `_base.py`.
   806→
   807→### 4. Event 15 Handler (Keep As-Is)
   808→
   809→The reactive handler from PR #899 stays. It covers:
   810→- Locks that don't have their PINs in the coordinator (edge cases)
   811→- Race conditions (code set at keypad between read and write)
   812→- Unmanaged slots with masked PINs
   813→
   814→## Out of Scope (Future TODOs)
   815→
   816→Captured in `TODO.md`:
   817→
   818→1. **Config flow lock reset step** — Check for unmanaged codes, require reset before
   819→   continuing
   820→2. **Conflicting integration detection** — Warn if Keymaster etc. is installed
   821→3. **Update migration docs** — Remove "slowly migrate" guidance
   822→4. **"Clear all unmanaged" UI action** — Bulk cleanup of stale codes
   823→5. **Lock-specific carve-outs** — Adapt detection per lock capability
   824→

"""Exceptions for lock_code_manager."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from homeassistant.exceptions import HomeAssistantError

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .providers import BaseLock


class LockCodeManagerError(HomeAssistantError):
    """Base class for lock_code_manager exceptions."""


class EntityNotFoundError(LockCodeManagerError):
    """Raise when en entity is not found."""

    def __init__(self, lock: BaseLock, slot_num: int, key: str):
        """Initialize the error."""
        self.lock = lock
        self.key = key
        self.slot_num = slot_num
        super().__init__(f"Entity not found for lock {lock} slot {slot_num} key {key}")


class LockDisconnected(LockCodeManagerError):
    """Raised when lock can't be communicated with."""


class ProviderNotImplementedError(LockCodeManagerError, NotImplementedError):
    """
    Raised when a provider method is not implemented.

    This exception should be raised by BaseLock methods that must be overridden
    by provider subclasses. It combines LockCodeManagerError (so the coordinator
    can catch it uniformly) with NotImplementedError (for standard Python semantics).
    """

    def __init__(self, provider: BaseLock, method_name: str, guidance: str = ""):
        """
        Initialize the error.

        Args:
            provider: The provider instance (self).
            method_name: Name of the method that needs to be implemented.
            guidance: Optional guidance on how to implement the method.

        """
        message = f"{provider.__class__.__name__} does not implement {method_name}()."
        if guidance:
            message = f"{message} {guidance}"
        super().__init__(message)

def _get_text_state(hass: HomeAssistant, entity_id: str | None) -> str | None:
    """Get text state from an entity, returning None if unavailable."""
    if not entity_id:
        return None
    if state := hass.states.get(entity_id):
        if state.state and state.state not in (STATE_UNKNOWN, STATE_UNAVAILABLE):
            return state.state
    return None


def _get_bool_state(hass: HomeAssistant, entity_id: str | None) -> bool | None:
    """Get boolean state from an entity, returning None if unavailable."""
    if not entity_id:
        return None
    if state := hass.states.get(entity_id):
        if state.state == STATE_ON:
            return True
        if state.state == STATE_OFF:
            return False
    return None


def _get_number_state(hass: HomeAssistant, entity_id: str | None) -> int | None:
    """Get integer state from a number entity, returning None if unavailable."""
    if not entity_id:
        return None
    if state := hass.states.get(entity_id):
        if state.state and state.state not in (STATE_UNKNOWN, STATE_UNAVAILABLE):
            try:
                return int(float(state.state))
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                return None
    return None


def _get_last_changed(
    hass: HomeAssistant, entity_id: str | None, require_valid_state: bool = False
) -> str | None:
    """
    Get last_changed timestamp as ISO string.

    Args:
        hass: Home Assistant instance.
        entity_id: Entity ID to get last_changed from.
        require_valid_state: If True, only return timestamp if state is not
            unknown/unavailable. Useful for event entities where last_changed
            only matters if the event has actually fired.

    """
    if not entity_id:
        return None
    if state := hass.states.get(entity_id):
        if require_valid_state and state.state in (STATE_UNKNOWN, STATE_UNAVAILABLE):
            return None
        if state.last_changed:
            return state.last_changed.isoformat()
    return None


# =============================================================================

def _find_config_entry_by_title(hass: HomeAssistant, title: str) -> ConfigEntry | None:
    """Find a config entry by title (slugified comparison)."""
    return next(
        (
            entry
            for entry in hass.config_entries.async_entries(DOMAIN)
            if slugify(entry.title) == slugify(title)
        ),
        None,
    )


def _get_slot_condition_entity_id(
    config_entry: ConfigEntry, slot_num: int
) -> str | None:
    """Get condition entity ID from slot config."""
    slots_data = get_entry_data(config_entry, CONF_SLOTS, {})
    slot_config = slots_data.get(slot_num) or slots_data.get(str(slot_num)) or {}
    if isinstance(slot_config, dict):
        return slot_config.get(CONF_ENTITY_ID)
    return None


def async_get_entry(
    orig_func: Callable[
        [HomeAssistant, websocket_api.ActiveConnection, dict[str, Any], ConfigEntry],
        Coroutine[Any, Any, None],
    ],
) -> Callable[
    [HomeAssistant, websocket_api.ActiveConnection, dict[str, Any]],
    Coroutine[Any, Any, None],
]:
    """Decorate async function to get entry."""

    @wraps(orig_func)
    async def async_get_entry_func(
        hass: HomeAssistant,
        connection: websocket_api.ActiveConnection,
        msg: dict[str, Any],
    ) -> None:
        """Provide user specific data and store to function."""
        if config_entry_title := msg.get("config_entry_title"):
            config_entry = _find_config_entry_by_title(hass, config_entry_title)
        elif config_entry_id := msg.get("config_entry_id"):
            config_entry = hass.config_entries.async_get_entry(config_entry_id)
        else:
            connection.send_error(
                msg["id"],
                websocket_api.const.ERR_INVALID_FORMAT,
                "Neither config_entry_title nor config_entry_id provided",
            )
            return

        if not config_entry:
            if config_entry_title:
                error_msg = (
                    f"No lock code manager config entry with title "
                    f"`{config_entry_title}` found"
                )
            else:
                error_msg = (
                    f"No lock code manager config entry with ID "
                    f"`{config_entry_id}` found"
                )
            connection.send_error(
                msg["id"],
                websocket_api.const.ERR_NOT_FOUND,
                error_msg,
            )
            return

        if config_entry.state is not ConfigEntryState.LOADED:
            connection.send_error(
                msg["id"],
                ERR_NOT_LOADED,
                f"Config entry {config_entry.entry_id} not loaded",
            )
            return

        await orig_func(hass, connection, msg, config_entry)

    return async_get_entry_func

    websocket_api.async_register_command(hass, get_config_entry_data)
    websocket_api.async_register_command(hass, subscribe_lock_codes)
    websocket_api.async_register_command(hass, subscribe_code_slot)
    websocket_api.async_register_command(hass, set_lock_usercode)
    websocket_api.async_register_command(hass, update_slot_condition)

    return True

        vol.Exclusive("config_entry_title", "entry"): str,
        vol.Exclusive("config_entry_id", "entry"): str,
